heading: Disclosure of certain matters occurring before grand jury
label: 3322 Disclosure of certain matters occurring before grand jury

children.text: received in the course of duty as an attorney for the government; or
disclosed under rule 6(e)(3)(A)(ii) of the Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure;

children.label: A
children.text: for use in relation to any matter within the jurisdiction of such regulatory agency; or

children.label: B
children.text: to assist an attorney for the government to whom matters have been disclosed under subsection (a).
A court may issue an order under paragraph (1) at any time during or after the completion of the investigation of the grand jury, upon a finding of a substantial need.

children.label: c
children.text: A person to whom matter has been disclosed under this section shall not use such matter other than for the purpose for which such disclosure was authorized.

children.label: B
children.text: section 1341 or 1343 affecting a financial institution; or

children.label: C
children.text: any provision of subchapter II of chapter 53 of title 31, United States Code;
the term “attorney for the government” has the meaning given such term in the Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure; and
the term “grand jury information” means matters occurring before a grand jury other than the deliberations of the grand jury or the vote of any grand juror.
